segments.text: We start on Screener, which gets us from ticker to business story without wasting motion.
segments.fallback_text: We open Screener and set the stage.
segments.subtitle: Step 1: Open Screener.in - India's popular stock research platform

segments.text: Rather than wander, we go straight to Reliance and start with a company most viewers already recognize.
segments.fallback_text: Straight to Reliance so the analysis starts fast.
segments.subtitle: Step 2: Navigate directly to Reliance Industries company page

segments.text: This first block is the trailer: size, valuation, and return ratios in one quick glance.
segments.fallback_text: This top block gives us the market snapshot.
segments.subtitle: Step 3: View key metrics - market cap, PE ratio, ROCE, and valuation snapshot

segments.text: Now we drop into the recent quarters, because the latest chapters matter more than the cover page.
segments.fallback_text: Quarterly numbers tell us what changed recently.
segments.subtitle: Step 4: Review quarterly financial trends to understand recent performance

segments.text: Profit and Loss tells us whether revenue is becoming a real business, not just a headline.
segments.fallback_text: Profit and Loss shows how sales turn into earnings.
segments.subtitle: Step 5: Inspect Profit and Loss details - revenue, expenses, and margins

segments.text: Then we check the balance sheet, where leverage and resilience stop being opinions.
segments.fallback_text: Balance sheet tells us how sturdy the company looks.
segments.subtitle: Step 6: Check balance sheet strength - assets, liabilities, and equity

segments.text: Now we switch from one-company analysis to idea generation, and that is where Public Screens earns its keep.
segments.fallback_text: Now we jump to Public Screens for idea generation.
segments.subtitle: Step 7: Open the Public Screens page to explore ready-made stock filters

segments.text: A public screen is basically a ready-made hypothesis. One click, and the market starts shortlisting itself.
segments.fallback_text: A public screen gives us a ready-made shortlist.
segments.subtitle: Step 8: Open a public screen to view companies matching prebuilt criteria

segments.text: Now the table becomes a comparison board: valuation, returns, and size sitting side by side.
segments.fallback_text: Here the table turns into a side-by-side comparison.
segments.subtitle: Step 9: Browse filtered companies and compare PE, ROE, and market cap

segments.text: From the shortlist, we open one company and turn a filter result into actual research.
segments.fallback_text: Open one result and go deeper.
segments.subtitle: Step 10: Open Ganesh Infra. from the screen for deeper analysis

segments.text: Final beat. We went from one company page to a reusable stock-research rhythm.
segments.subtitle: Step 11: Tutorial complete - you can now research and compare stocks on Screener.in
